summary: Added a dependency-free madobe-transport crate with an in-memory QUIC-shaped loopback session, a framed video sample lane, typed transport errors, deterministic send/receive telemetry timestamps, focused loopback tests, and evidence that avoids live-network claims.

acceptanceEvidence.criterion: Loopback sender and receiver tests pass.
Video lane carries framed sample payloads.
acceptanceEvidence.evidence: crates/transport/src/lib.rs defines VideoSample and VideoLane::send/receive; crates/transport/tests/loopback.rs asserts a sample round trip.

acceptanceEvidence.criterion: Telemetry records send and receive timestamps.
acceptanceEvidence.evidence: crates/transport/tests/loopback.rs video_lane_records_send_and_receive_timestamps asserts send and receive TransportTelemetryEvent timestamps.

acceptanceEvidence.criterion: No live network claim is made without cross-device validation.
acceptanceEvidence.evidence: crates/transport/src/lib.rs crate docs and evidence/m3-quic-transport-skeleton/notes.md state this is loopback-only with no socket I/O or cross-device claim.

commandsRun.command: nix develop -c cargo clippy -p madobe-transport --all-targets --all-features -- -D warnings

realWorldValidation.evidence: Skeleton-only loopback node; no live network or cross-device validation was performed or claimed.

residualRisks: Real QUIC networking is not implemented in this node.
Cross-device send/receive validation is deferred to a later live transport validation node.
The loopback transport uses in-memory queues and does not model packet loss, congestion, stream scheduling, TLS, or real QUIC handshakes.
Payload hashes are carried from protocol metadata but not cryptographically verified by this skeleton.
